## [00:00 - 03:00]

Okay, so now let's say you've got a partner on board, maybe you've got more than one partner on board. Now, one really important thing, and this is something that people don't see going, that sort of happens behind the scenes, is the importance of you putting together a sequence for your partners. In other words, this isn't a sequence to sell your product, this is a sequence to sell your partners on promoting your product and promoting it really hard. So, it's one thing for them to mail, to send out one email for your product, for your pre-launch, sending people to your first piece of pre-launch content. It's another thing for them to go all out for your launch. And that's why you want to put together this sequence. This sequence is going to use all the triggers I've already taught you, or maybe not all of them, but many of the triggers, the mental triggers that I've already taught you in product launch formula. Generally, the sequence is almost always done via email. Sometimes you might create a video for your partners, or maybe even two videos for your partners, but it's not like a pre-launch sequence for them in terms of the opportunity video and that type of thing. But it's a lot of the same mental triggers, it's a lot of the same psychology. So, you've already learned what you need. Like I said, it's one thing to get them to mail once, it's another thing to get them to mail over and over. My top partners, and in our business, we do this better than anyone. And so this is that I'm going to actually walk you through my sequence, the sequence I use to get people excited to mail for me and keep them mailing. You know, those part, of course, you know those partners that mail eight or 10 times during my launch, they're not doing it out of their goodness or their heart, they're doing it because they're going to make a lot of money, they make tons of commissions, my products convert, they make tons of commissions, they help a lot of products, they align themselves with me and my brand, which is, you know, I think the most powerful brand in the market, in my market. ## [03:00 - 06:01]

That's the big promise of my prelaunch and why they should really get on board. So like, you know, the mechanics of this JV launch is basically your partners are going to be mailing into your squeeze page. And you want them to mail each piece of prelaunch content. What you want them to do is you want them to mail into that first piece, but you don't want them to stop there. ## [06:01 - 09:04]

But it comes and goes. The best, there is no best software. There's no perfect software. Those are just some of the solutions out there now. But whatever software you use, it's basically tracking people all the way through. So when you make the sale, you're able to pay the Joint Venture Partner that sent them to you. Now, one of the things is Joint Venture Launches are just inherently messier than your internal launches. Because this is your ideal partner, right? OK, so that's ideal partner right there. But then, you're always going to have your other, here's another partner for you. And they don't actually come out. They don't have mail your first. They're not even part of the launch. But then, they're watching the market. They're watching other people mail. And they see what's happening. And they notice you here with your first piece of pre-launch content. And they start to get excited about your second piece. And they get ahold of you. And they say, hey, I want to promote for you. And you're like, OK, great. You're four days into your pre-launch already. And you're about to release PLC3. Now, they might just come on board and mail your third piece and mail it your sales video. Are you going to refuse not let them do that? No, you're going to let them do that. But the thing is, is they're going to send their people into the third piece of pre-launch content. Or they might elect the male people into your first piece. But now, they're coming in later. And it's sort of a catch up. And that's what I mean when I say that it's inherently messier. If you're doing an internal launch, you mail your list for each one of these. You might be mailing your list more than one time for each one of those. But with a joint venture launch, you can't assume that everyone will get, your partners will mail for each piece. So inherently messier. Now, your number one job when you're in a JV launch is making money for your partners. If you go back to that first video where I was talking about joint ventures, I mentioned that. You don't want to practice on your joint venture partners. That's why you want to do the internal launch first. You want to make money for your partners. Don't screw this up. Don't test out an offer on them. Don't test out a sales video on them. Take care of them. Treat their list like gold. I mean, think about how important your list is to you. And I want you to take their list, even more carefully, than you treat your list. Because this is a leverage situation. If you have all these different JVs, you got a whole bunch of JVs. Maybe you got five of them. And collectively, they might have whatever. They got 100,000 people or 200,000 people on their list. You have to treat those lists like gold. So you have to put out great pre-launch content. And you got to make sure that your launch converts. Don't mess this up. Because if they mail for you and you don't convert for them

## [09:04 - 12:05]

and you don't make them money, they're not going to mail for you the next time around. The word gets around. In any market, all these partners, they're all talking to each other. They might, and when they're talking to each other and your launch doesn't convert, you're not going to, they're not going to mail for you again going down the road. So take care of your partners. Treat their list like gold. Just because it's not your list doesn't mean you can abuse it. You got to give them a great pre-launch. You got to give them a great sales video and make sure you convert. That's why I say you don't want to start out with a joint venture launch. Now every now and then, I do see someone that's their first launch as a JV launch. And it works out, but it's not ideal. I mean, there's always going to be a few exceptions to the rules, a few superstars. And it'll work for them. But for the most part, you want to start off with an internal launch first. Now the thing is, just putting the whole process together is a lot of work. Putting together an internal launch is plenty of work. But the thing is, a joint venture launch is literally twice as much work as a regular. So your internal launch is right here, OK? Boom. We're going to go with the red here. So this is, here's your internal launch. It's just putting together your pre-launch content, your sales video. And that's a lot of work. So let's just call that work X. There's X amount of work. Managing this piece is 2X. So there is as much work right here managing a joint venture partners as there is putting together the pre-launch. And that's another reason why you want to do that internal launch first. You just, you got to get it right. You have to get this piece right. And you don't want to come out with an offer that doesn't convert for people. Then there's lots and lots of small, little decisions about this, about, in a JV launch. Little, tiny things like how you set the cookies, and how you track, and how you follow up. And you, in each one of those decisions, you can either favor your affiliate or favor your pocketbook. And I'm not going to claim I'm perfect, but I know within my team, my organization, we make those decisions almost every time for the affiliate. And when you do that and treat your affiliates well and make them a lot of money, then they're going to come back and promote for you. And that's why we do so well going forward. We just keep on doing one great JV launch after another, because we really take care of our partners. We really sweat it. We just make sure we follow up for those people. After the launch, we keep on promoting to those people and keep on paying affiliates. We always pay on time. Ever since 2005, we have paid on time every single month. We track like bloodhounds. We go to great lengths to try to make sure we track every sale to an affiliate. That's how you win in the long run.

## [12:05 - 13:59]

And I see all kinds. Like one time I saw a launch where they actually, the day before their launch, they sent people to a final video. And when you landed on the page, right on the top of the page there's a big red thing that said, join our affiliate program. And so I'm mailing on this. And all of a sudden, I'm mailing. And the first thing people see, big and red on the top of the page is join our affiliate program. So everyone that I'm sending as a video, all the people on my list I'm sending there, they end up joining the affiliate program. Then the next day, right when they opened up, an hour before they opened up, they sent an email out to their affiliates that said, oh, by the way, you're allowed to buy through your affiliate link. And essentially, that meant people could buy and get paid for an affiliate commission on their own purchase. So they were able to get, I don't know, if it was 50% commission, they were able to get half off. So they not only told people, let my visitors, they not only gave them this offer to join their affiliate program, but then the next day, they said buy through your affiliate link and get half off. So all those people bought through their own link, got half off, and they didn't buy through my link. That's a way to screw over your partners, screw over your affiliates. If you think about it really hard, you can go with all different kinds of ways to screw over your affiliates. And the more that you do that, well, it's just a one-time deal. You're not gonna do well going forward if you screw over your affiliates. You gotta take care of your affiliates. Your number one job in a JV launch is taking care of your partners and your affiliates.
